name: androguard

documentation.readme: Do you think your phone has been pwned ? please check IsMyPhonePwned
usage_sections: Installation: Quick installation: pip install androguard > [!IMPORTANT] > Versions >= 4.0.0 are new releases after a long time, where the project has substantial differences from the previous stable version 3.3.5 from 2019. This means that certain functionalities have been removed. If you noti

Features: Androguard is a full python tool to play with Android files. DEX, ODEX APK Android's binary xml Android resources Disassemble DEX/ODEX bytecodes Basic Decompiler for DEX/ODEX files Frida support for easy dynamic analysis SQLite database to save the session

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

function_signatures.description: Pairs with the Penetration Testing system-prompt stanza in core.ai_backend.chain to teach the LLM the standard invocation pattern.

function_signatures.name: operator_initiated_the_chain_step_is_gated_by_the_per_step_accept_cancel_gate_the_tool_does_not_auto_execute
function_signatures.description: Operator-initiated: the chain step is gated by the per-step ACCEPT/CANCEL gate; the tool does not auto-execute.

function_signatures.description: Top-level CLI runner for androguard. Returns a result envelope (ok, data, error).

examples: Androguard + tools: Anthony Desnos (desnos at t0t0.fr).

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with volatilityfoundation/volatility3 (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with unknown/drozer (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with unknown/Mobile-Security-Framework-MobSF (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Phase 3: same attack_surface=['shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 4: same attack_surface=['shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 5: same attack_surface=['shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [shell_linux] in the Penetration Testing category. Fits when the engagement needs: ![banner](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/androguard/androguard/master/assets/web/androguardwithname.jpg).
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Do you think your phone has been pwned ? please check IsMyPhonePwned.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: pip install androguard. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `Androguard + tools: Anthony Desnos (desnos at t0t0.fr).`.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Penetration Testing, use androguard because ![banner](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/androguard/androguard/master/assets/web/androguardwithname.jpg).
When you need a concrete invocation, run `Androguard + tools: Anthony Desnos (desnos at t0t0.fr).` as documented upstream for androguard.
When chaining Penetration Testing tools, pair androguard with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `androguard --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
command_examples: Androguard + tools: Anthony Desnos (desnos at t0t0.fr).
